what_it_does: Provides headless, programmatic read and compact operations for an existing supervised live session by injecting the /context and /compact slash commands into the session-supervisor and capturing the declared events it emits.
observations: Uses urllib to talk to a session-supervisor HTTP service at COMPANY_SUPERVISOR_BASE (default 127.0.0.1:8771).
Captures the supervisor event stream via /watch in a daemon thread, timestamps each event by arrival time, and filters only events arriving after the /inject call.
read_context injects /context and looks for a system/local_command_output declared event.
compact_session injects /compact [focus] and looks for a system/compact_boundary declared event.
Implements a short quiet-drain period before injecting to avoid losing the inject response in a backlog flood.
Returns result dictionaries rather than raising on missing events (but raises ContextOpError if the supervisor is unreachable or inject is refused).
standouts: Arrival-time filtering to isolate exactly the current turn's events and avoid stale /watch backlog.
Headless reuse of TUI-only slash commands by injecting them into a live session.
Waiting for a system/compact_boundary marker as the compaction completion signal.
conventions: Docstrings cite lead verification dates and prior probe files.
Environment-variable override for service base URL.
JSON request bodies with explicit Content-Type.
Daemon thread for background streaming reader.
concerns: Hard-coded supervisor endpoints (/health, /watch, /inject) with no schema validation.
Quiet-drain heuristic could still miss events in a very high-rate stream.
If the supervisor returns non-JSON on HTTP error, json.loads will raise a secondary exception.
No validation or sanitization of session id or focus string.
Thread stop event is set after the loop, but exceptions in run() are silently swallowed.
notes: A thin orchestration layer that turns the supervisor's slash-command/event-stream machinery into a remote context-window read/compact API.
questions: What is the exact line format of the /watch stream (SSE, newline-delimited JSON, etc.)?
What fields does system/local_command_output reliably contain?
How does the supervisor handle an invalid or empty session id?
What guarantees that memory survives compaction beyond the lead-verified note?
What does the done event's result field contain when no declared event is captured?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it injects slash commands and captures declared events to expose /context and /compact headlessly.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud orchestration over a live session
intention_signals: The file tries to bridge external automation with the in-process TUI context controls by treating slash commands as an API surface and the supervisor's declared events as the response channel.
novelty: Using slash-command injection plus arrival-time-filtered event capture to expose TUI-only /context and /compact operations to headless callers.
contribution: A reusable, small set of functions for reading context usage and triggering compaction on a live supervised session, plus an event-capture helper that timestamps and isolates the current turn's events.
summary_for_embedding: A Python module that injects /context and /compact slash commands into a running session-supervisor session and captures declared events to provide headless context-window read and compact operations.
intention_for_embedding: To let external automation query and compact a live session's context window by reusing the supervisor's existing slash-command and event-stream machinery.

symbols.does: Exception class raised when a context op cannot run.
symbols.description: A RuntimeError subclass used to make supervisor reachability and injection failures explicit rather than silent no-ops.

symbols.does: Sends a JSON HTTP request to the supervisor and returns status plus parsed body.
symbols.description: A small urllib helper that builds a request, sends it, and returns a (status, parsed-json) tuple, including parsing the body of HTTP errors.

symbols.does: Injects a message into a live session and captures matching declared events plus the turn done marker.
symbols.description: Opens a streaming /watch reader in a daemon thread, performs a quiet backlog drain, posts the message to /inject, then collects only events that arrived after the inject timestamp until a done event or timeout.

symbols.does: Background thread that reads the /watch stream and records timestamped events.
symbols.description: A nested daemon function that keeps the /watch HTTP response open, parses each line as JSON, stores (arrival_time, event) tuples, and exits when the stop event is set.

symbols.does: Checks that the session-supervisor is reachable via /health.
symbols.description: A small guard that pings the supervisor health endpoint and raises ContextOpError with a startup hint if it cannot connect.

symbols.does: Injects /context and returns the captured usage breakdown.
symbols.description: Public function that triggers the context usage command, waits for a system/local_command_output declared event, and returns a dictionary containing the session id, operation name, observation flag, and truncated raw output.

symbols.does: Injects /compact and waits for the system/compact_boundary marker.
symbols.description: Public function that triggers a compaction with optional focus text, waits for the compact boundary declared event, and reports whether the boundary was seen and the turn completed.
